headline: STRAIT OF HORMUZ STALEMATE HAMMERS METALS — Gold Futures Drop $44 to $4,708 as Israel Rules Out Resolution
snippet: Gold futures shed $44 to $4,708/oz and silver dropped $2.51 to $75.44 as Israel signalled no intention of ending the conflict or reopening the Strait of Hormuz, closing out the week with gold down over 2%. Crude oil climbed toward $97/bbl, stoking inflation fears that are complicating the safe-haven thesis and weighing on non-yielding bullion.

headline: SILVER DEFIES BUST — After 149% Parabolic Run, ETF Buying at Second-Highest Record Holds the Floor
snippet: Adam Hamilton's latest analysis finds silver maintaining elevated price levels despite expectations of a sharp post-parabolic correction. Silver-ETF holdings surged 312% year-over-year to 278.1 million ounces — the second-highest inflow on record by volume and highest by value — forming a structural demand floor. Hamilton argues a new $70–$80 price regime is consolidating rather than collapsing.

headline: UR-ENERGY FLIPS THE SWITCH — Second U.S. Uranium ISR Mine Goes Live at Wyoming's Shirley Basin
snippet: Ur-Energy (URG) has commenced uranium solution extraction from Mine Unit 1 at Shirley Basin, Wyoming, the company's second ISR project, bringing combined licensed production capacity at Lost Creek and Shirley Basin to 4.2 million lbs U₃O₈ per year. Loaded resin is expected to be transported to Lost Creek for processing this summer pending a final regulatory inspection.

headline: DISCOVERY SILVER HITS 770 G/T GOLD AT PORCUPINE — 143-Hole Drill Campaign Confirms Multi-Zone Extension
snippet: Discovery Silver (TSX: DSV) released 143-hole assay results from its Porcupine complex, with standout intercepts including 72.46 g/t Au over 3.8m (incl. 770 g/t over 0.3m) in the TVZ zone, 45.77 g/t over 4.1m at Hoyle Pond South, and 24.11 g/t over 8.0m at Pamour West — all confirming growth potential across multiple zones ahead of a late-2026 mineral resource update.

headline: DISCOVERY SILVER TAKES 9.9% STRATEGIC STAKE IN ABITIBI METALS — C$30.75M Financing Signals Bold Bet on Abitibi Gold
snippet: Abitibi Metals Corp. is raising up to C$30.75M in a non-brokered private placement with Discovery Silver Corp. (TSX: DSV) expected to acquire approximately 9.9% on a non-diluted basis. The deal includes 11.76M flow-through shares at $0.85 and 35.78M hard-dollar shares at $0.58. Discovery Silver also receives participation rights to maintain its stake, signalling serious interest in Abitibi's gold-copper assets in Quebec's Abitibi greenstone belt.

headline: COMEX COPPER STOCKPILES HIT ALL-TIME RECORD as U.S. Arbitrage Pulls Metal from Global Markets
snippet: Total COMEX copper warehouse stocks climbed to a record 603,745 short tons as traders exploited renewed U.S.-global price spreads. Spot copper held near $6.02/lb (April 24), cushioned by Chinese restocking ahead of the May 1–5 Labor Day holiday. Chinese smelters set a production record of 1.33 million tonnes in March.

headline: RIO2 STARTS $27M TAILINGS FACILITY BUILD-OUT AT PERU COPPER MINE — Water Recovery to Jump from 60% to 90%
snippet: Rio2 (TSX: RIO) has entered the commissioning phase for the new dry-stack tailings filtration facility at the Condestable copper mine in Peru. The 8,400 t/d facility — an 18-month, $27M capital project — will recover nearly 90% of process water and underpin throughput expansion ambitions. Full commercial operation is targeted for Q3 2026.

headline: MELKIOR DRILLS FOR BONANZA GOLD NEAR TIMMINS — 3,000-Meter Program Targets High-Grade Zamzam-Jowsey Zone
snippet: Melkior Resources (TSXV: MKR) has mobilised for a 10-hole, 3,000-meter drill program at its 100%-owned Carscallen Gold Project 25 km west of Timmins, Ontario. The campaign follows up high-grade 1010 Zone intercepts and pursues step-outs along the bonanza-grade Zamzam-Jowsey structure. First assay results are expected by end of June 2026.

headline: CRITICAL METALS LOCKS DOWN 92.5% OF WORLD'S LARGEST HEAVY RARE EARTH DEPOSIT — Tanbreez, Greenland
snippet: After Greenland approved the final 50.5% licence transfer, Critical Metals Corp (CRML) now controls 92.5% of the Tanbreez project — one of the world's largest untapped HREE deposits, carrying all eight heavy rare earths critical for defence, EVs, and clean energy. The stock surged 23.6% on the news. The company holds a $120M U.S. EXIM Bank letter of intent and has pilot plant operations planned for May 2026.

headline: CULLINAN METALS OPTIONS LABRADOR RARE EARTH PROJECT IN CANADA'S EMERGING CRITICAL REE DISTRICT
snippet: Cullinan Metals (CSE: CMT) has optioned the Oculus Rare Earth Project (2,550 ha) in Central Labrador's recognized Critical REE district and the Burnt Pond VMS project in Newfoundland. Historical samples at Oculus returned high-grade REE values in a district 110 km northeast of Churchill Falls. Total option payments are $675,000 over two years.
